scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT RELATING TO IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant regarding the residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, as per the original Agreement to Sell marked as Annexure A.

6. The total consideration agreed upon for the said property was Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs only).

7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Lakhs only) to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS, evidenced by the bank receipt bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654, marked as Annexure B.

8. The balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against execution of the sale deed, and the Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract.

BREACH PARTICULARS

9. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the sale deed, claiming that he had received a higher offer for the property, a copy of which is marked as Annexure C.

10. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed as per the Agreement, marked as Annexure D.

11. Despite the said notice and the Plaintiff's readiness to perform, the Defendant has refused to perform his obligations under the Agreement to Sell.

DAMAGES

12. By reason of the Defendant's wrongful refusal and breach of contract, the Plaintiff has suffered prejudice and is entitled to compensation for the loss incurred, in addition to the relief of specific performance and costs of this suit.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The present cause is governed by Specific Relief Act, 1963, including Section 10 (mandatory after 2018 Amendment — court SHALL grant unless S.11(2), S.14, or S.16 exception), Section 16, Section 21, and Section 22.

14. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 10.

15. The Plaintiff also relies on Transfer of Property Act, 1882, including Section 53A, where the pleaded facts attract those provisions.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

17. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

18. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

20. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for specific performance of the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024, directing the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration;
    (b) Award damages for the breach of contract in the event specific performance is not granted;
    (c) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 18 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. {{SUIT_NO}} / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership,
Having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

2. The suit is valued above the specified value of Rs. 3,00,000/- as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

3. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

4. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

5. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when the contract is broken; or for continuing breach, when it ceases. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

7. The Plaintiff has complied with the mandatory requirement of pre-institution mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. [Mediation Certificate Details: {{MEDIATION_CERT_NO}}].

FACTS OF THE CASE

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system, for a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is annexed hereto as **Annexure A**.

9. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant as the first milestone payment on 01.02.2024 via NEFT, bearing UTR Number ICIC20240201456789. A copy of the NEFT receipt is annexed hereto as **Annexure B**.

10. As per the agreed timeline, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

11. Upon the Defendant's failure to perform, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding performance and refund. A copy of the said legal notice is annexed hereto as **Annexure E**.

BREACH PARTICULARS

12. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the stipulated deadline of 01.05.2024, thereby committing a breach of the Agreement.

13. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant unilaterally abandoned the project without completing the development work or delivering any module, as evidenced by the email correspondence between the parties. A copy of the email correspondence is annexed hereto as **Annexure C**.

14. Despite the abandonment of the project and the issuance of the legal notice, the Defendant has failed and refused to refund the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- received from the Plaintiff.

DAMAGES

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant.

16. Due to the non-delivery and delay in operations caused by the Defendant's breach, the Plaintiff has suffered consequential losses amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/-, as assessed in the auditor's report. A copy of the loss assessment report is annexed hereto as **Annexure D**.

17. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the outstanding amounts from the date of breach until realization.

LEGAL BASIS

18. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

20. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

21. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

22. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

23. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

24. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of judgment / decree till the date of realisation of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) towards damages;
    (b) Award Rs. 20,00,000/- towards Actual Loss (Refund);
    (c) Award Rs. 12,00,000/- towards Consequential loss / damages;
    (d) Award interest at 18% per annum on the outstanding amounts;
    (e) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (f) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (g) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

25. Actual Loss (Refund)
   Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
   Basis / methodology: Advance payment made under Agreement dated 01.02.2024
   Documentary proof: Annexure B (NEFT Receipt)

26. Consequential loss / damages
   Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
   Basis / methodology: Loss assessment report by auditor due to operational delay
   Documentary proof: Annexure D (Loss Assessment Report)

27. Interest On Delayed Payment
   Amount claimed: As per prayer (18% p.a.)
   Basis / methodology: Contractual breach and equitable jurisdiction
   Documentary proof: N/A

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. 32,00,000/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Softwa
